description: You are a Senior Auditor at a health insurance company. Using the CLAIMS schema in Oracle (claims_analyst/Claims2024 to XEPDB1), implement the fraud detection rules described in /home/ga/Desktop/fraud_detection_spec.txt. Create a PL/SQL package named FRAUD_DETECTION_PKG containing a pipelined table function BENFORD_ANALYSIS for first-digit distribution analysis, a function FIND_STATISTICAL_OUTLIERS for identifying claims exceeding 3 standard deviations from provider means, and procedures DETECT_DUPLICATES and DETECT_UPCODING. Results must be stored in a table named FRAUD_FLAGS (including columns for claim_id, flag_type, severity, detection_date, and details) and summarized in a materialized view named FRAUD_SUMMARY_MV. Export the summary report to /home/ga/fraud_report.csv with columns FLAG_TYPE, SEVERITY, FLAG_COUNT, and TOTAL_AMOUNT_FLAGGED. Connection details: Host=localhost, Port=1521, Service=XEPDB1, User=claims_analyst, Password=Claims2024.

fraud_types_to_detect.duplicates: Same patient + procedure + date within 7 days
fraud_types_to_detect.upcoding: Provider procedure mix differs > 2 stddev from population
